TPM Benchmark Signal Room
Thesis Runtime Authoring Evaluation Results Autopsy Diagrams
Deterministic TPM Benchmark Harness

Hold the org constant. Vary the TPM.

This dashboard turns your harness into a review-session control room: benchmark thesis up front, runtime mechanics in the middle, and bundle-level fingerprints plus a run autopsy at the end.

Live Artifact Readout

Loading benchmark artifacts…

Control Surface

Benchmark-first framing

The dashboard is designed to reinforce your thesis before you ever show a score: this is a controlled TPM benchmark, not a roleplay demo.

World Mechanics

Discrete-event week, concrete observability

The runtime advances when the TPM acts and when scheduled events fire. The hard part of TPM work is not just what is true, but the gap between truth, artifacts, and belief.

Policy Coworkers

Authored actors instead of runtime improv

    State Triad

    Partial observability is first-class

    Authoring Firewall

    Model-assisted creation, frozen runtime execution

    Models can help draft and fill a candidate world offline, but the authoritative gates stay deterministic: validation, closure, and scoring.

    Calibration Probes

    Closure suite as benchmark certification

    The point of the closure suite is not to flatter the environment. It is to prove that strong, weak, noisy, and theater-heavy behaviors separate in deterministic ways.

    Evidence Engine

    State-derived scoring that resists reward hacking

    Rubric lines attach to predicates over state and evidence. A doc, note, or meeting only matters if it changes belief, commitment, readiness, or milestone truth.

    Counts

    High-signal TPM behavior

      Does Not Count

      Activity without leverage

        Rubric Decoder

        Actual score groups, fixed weights, and scenario line items

        Line Item Ledger

        What the evaluator explicitly checks

        Results Lab

        Bundle fingerprints over raw logs

        This is the showpiece section for review: switch scenarios, compare models, and surface the patterns that matter before dropping into any single trace.

        Score Skyline

        Mean, range, and stability by model

        TPM Fingerprint

        Competency profile across the bundle

        Clue Conversion Lattice

        Surfacing vs turning cues into plan changes

        Failure Fault Lines

        Recurring bundle-level root causes

        Bundle Seed Tableau

        Seed-by-seed run shape

        Run Autopsy

        One representative episode, opened up

        Use this section right after the bundle view. It makes the summary tangible without drowning the room in raw logs.

        Diagram Deck

        Pull your supporting visuals into the same room

        These are wired in from the repo so the dashboard complements your Excalidraw deck instead of replacing it.
